title: Beilschmiedia pustulata
taxonomy: Beilschmiedia pustulata is classified within the family Lauraceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as laurel or lauraceae. The genus Beilschmiedia is a part of this family and is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Beilschmiedia pustulata, specifically, is a species of tree native to New Caledonia, an island country in the Pacific Ocean. It belongs to the order Laurales and the class Magnoliopsida, which includes flowering plants that have a two-lobed cotyledon. The species is also known by its other names, but the exact classification is within the Lauraceae family.
